data.title: Hygeia Healthcare's Co-CEO Steps Down After Redesignation; Successor Named
data.description:
Cheng Huanhuan stepped down as Hygeia Healthcare's co-chief executive officer due to a redesignation of duties in the group, a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

Cheng Huanhuan will remain as an executive director and has been appointed as the secretary to the board.

The oncology healthcare group named executive director Ren Ai as the new co-CEO.

data.title: Euro Area Economic Sentiment Unexpectedly Worsens
data.description:
The Eurozone’s Economic Sentiment Indicator fell to 95.2 in March 2025, the lowest level in three months, compared to 96.3 in February and well below forecasts of 97.

The decline was driven by lower confidence in services (2.4 vs 5.1), retail trade (-6.8 vs -5.1) and among consumers (-14.5 vs -13.6).

Confidence in industry (-10.6 vs -11) and construction (-3.4 vs -3.3) remained broadly stable.

For the largest EU economies, the ESI declined significantly in France (-2.1 to 96.4) and Italy (-2.0 to 97.6), while it improved in Spain (+1.1 to 103.4).

Confidence edged up in Germany (+0.3 to 89.4) and remained unchanged in the Netherlands (at 100.3).

data.title: Italy Industrial Sales Rebound
data.description:
Industrial sales in Italy jumped 3.8% month-on-month in January 2025, recovering strongly from a 2.7% drop in the previous month.

Sales increased in both the domestic market (3.9%) and the foreign market (3.6%).

Compared to the same month last year, industrial sales rose 1.7%.

data.title: Italian Monthly Industrial Sales Up 3.8% in January
data.description:
Italy's industrial turnover rose 3.8% month over month in January, after a 2.7% fall a month ago, according to data from Istat published Friday.

On a yearly basis, industrial sales were 1.7% higher, against the 7.2% decrease earlier.

data.title: Polyethylene terephthalate (PET) contract prices climb in Europe
data.description: This week, PET contract prices increased, while spot prices remained flat in the European region.An industry source in Europe informed a Polymerupdate team member, "European PET contract prices strengthened in March, driven by a consistently tight feedstock market and a shift in trading focus towards contract agreements instead of the spot market. The increased dependence on imports, coupled with interruptions in domestic production, exerted upward pressure on prices. As a result, March contract prices increased compared to February, as producers aimed to transfer higher feedstock costs to customers, particularly for Purified terephthalic acid (PTA) and Monoethylene glycol (MEG), both of which remained in limited supply. The constraints on domestic production, due to both planned and unplanned outages, compelled buyers to secure materials through long-term contracts, thereby diminishing liquidity in the spot market. Additionally, the tightness in feedstock, along with disruptions in freight and logistics�such as extended lead times for imported materials�further contributed to the rise in prices. Asian imports, which usually alleviate supply pressures in Europe, faced fluctuating freight rates and heightened competition from demand in other regions. Market participants observed steady demand in the beverage and packaging industries, with some seasonal restocking in anticipation of peak summer consumption. However, overarching macroeconomic concerns and cautious purchasing behavior among downstream buyers moderated aggressive buying activity. Looking forward, the outlook for PET pricing in April will hinge on trends in feedstock prices, the stability of supply chains, and any potential recovery in regional production capacity. Market participants will closely watch producers' strategies for passing on costs and the effects of any new developments on the supply side. In the contract markets, bottle grade PET prices were assessed at the Euro 1145-1150/mt FD NWE Germany and FD NWE Italy levels, both rising by Euro (+15/mt) week on week. PET bottle grade prices were assessed at the Euro 1145-1150/mt FD NWE France levels, a gain of Euro (+15/mt) from the previous week. Bottle grade PET prices were also assessed at the GBP 925-930/mt FD NWE UK levels, unchanged week on week.In the spot markets, FD North West Europe PET prices were assessed at the Euro 1055-1065/mt levels, stable from last week. The Source added, "Trading activity in the European spot Polyethylene terephthalate (PET) market has remained consistent, even in the face of signs of weakened demand. Participants in the market have noted stable transaction levels throughout the region, although buyer confidence has been affected by the fluctuations of the US dollar against the euro, which has influenced purchasing choices. In spite of this currency instability, a substantial influx of Asian PET is projected to reach Europe in April, coinciding with the onset of the preform manufacturing season. Some Asian producers are offering their products at reduced prices, making imports an appealing option for European buyers in search of more economical alternatives. Although demand fundamentals are still lacklustre in certain sectors, the expected rise in import volumes may impact pricing trends in the upcoming months. Market participants are closely observing exchange rate changes, shipping costs, and the shifting supply-demand landscape as critical elements influencing market sentiment. Feedstock MEG spot prices were assessed at the Euro 740-745/mt FD Northwest Europe levels, while PTA prices were assessed at the USD 729-730/mt FD Northwest Europe levels, both rolled over from the previous week.In Asia, PET prices were assessed at the USD 780-800/mt FOB North East Asia levels, left unchanged from last week.

data.description:

data.title: Euro Area Economic Sentiment Down in March
data.description:
Economic sentiment in the euro area declined in March, with the indicator down to 95.2 points from 96.3 points in February, according to European Commission data published Friday.

The latest reading is below the consensus estimate of 97 points.

Meanwhile, the consumer confidence index was confirmed at -14.5 points, down from -13.6 points previously.

data.title: Major brokerages see slower pace of Fed rate cuts amid Trump tariff uncertainty
data.description:
Major brokerages still expect a slower pace of interest-rate cuts by the U.S. Federal Reserve ahead of the personal consumption expenditures (PCE) data, the central bank's preferred measure of inflation, due later in the day.

February's PCE numbers are anticipated to reveal a rebound in consumer spending and a rise in annual core PCE prices to 2.7%.

Meanwhile, U.S. President Donald Trump unveiled a 25% tariff on imported cars and light trucks set to take effect on April 3, forcing further uncertainty in U.S. financial markets.

Currently, traders expect two rate cuts of 25 basis points each for the year, according to data compiled by LSEG.

data.title: Bearish pressure from surging wind supply expected on Monday
data.description:
German and French prompt power prices for Monday were untraded on Friday, but a sharp rise in wind power supply and a slight fall in demand were seen putting bearish pressure on the start of the next week.

Both the German and French Monday baseload power prices were untraded by 1002 GMT, LSEG data showed. (TRDEBD3), (TRFRBD3)

Residual load is decreasing throughout the region on Monday, LSEG analyst Florine Enengl said, as demand is seen down and wind supply in Germany is up.

German wind power output was expected to jump by 13.8 gigawatts (GW) to 23.6 GW on Monday, while that from France was projected to edge up 240 megawatts (MW) to 5 GW, according to LSEG data.

German solar power supply was expected to record a strong fall of 6.2 GW to 8.3 GW, the data showed.

The year is still young, but Asia has already built up a considerable lead over several European countries and the United States in terms of energy transition progress so far in 2025.

French nuclear availability rose two percentage points to 76% of total capacity.

Power consumption in Germany is forecast to slide 180 MW to 55.7 GW, while demand in France is expected to fall 2.7 GW to 50.4 GW on Monday, LSEG data showed.

German year-ahead power (TRDEBYZ6) fell 0.4% to 84.35 euros/MWh, while the French 2025 baseload contract (TRFRBYZ6) was untraded with a bid-ask range between 62.20 euros and 62.40 euros/MWh.

In the European carbon market (CFI2Zc1), the benchmark contract rose 0.2% to 68.68 euros a metric ton.

data.title: Market Chatter: EU Expected to Impose Minimal Fines on Apple, Meta
data.description:
The European Union will reportedly apply digital rules on Apple and Meta Platforms but limit its fines on the US tech giants to stave off any potential trade retaliation from the US government.

The European Commission is expected to fine and order Apple to change its App Store rules under its Digital Markets Act, London's Financial Times reported Friday. It will also close a separate investigation into Apple over its web browser choice design.

Meanwhile, Meta is said to receive a penalty and be required to change its model forcing users to either accept data tracking or pay for an ad-free experience.

The European Commission declined to comment when reached by MT Newswires.
